commands.review_summary:
The PR updates the Codex app-server plugin inventory, activation, app inventory cache, thread config refresh path, protocol typing, and focused tests so configured plugin-owned apps survive a missing local inventory and remote curated plugins use Codex remote IDs.

PR surface: Source +51, Tests +126. Total +177 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main can miss remote-only curated plugin summaries and can keep plugin app exposure fail-closed when the OpenClaw process-local inventory is missing. I did not run a live repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The protected-label PR has no narrow code defect for automation to repair; maintainers should review the compatibility proof gap and CI state.

target.title: fix(codex): keep plugin apps available when inventory is missing
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users starting a Codex-backed OpenClaw thread could lose configured plugin-owned apps when OpenClaw's process-local app inventory was missing. It also fixes remote curated plugins failing detail or activation calls when Codex requires the backend remote plugin ID rather than the display name.

## Why This Change Was Made

Thread startup now refreshes a missing app inventory before building the app config, paginating until the configured app IDs are found or the inventory is exhausted and rejecting repeated cursors instead of caching a known-partial result. Remote curated plugin reads and installs consistently use Codex's `remotePluginId`, and marketplace selection searches each accepted curated marketplace for the requested plugin instead of assuming the first curated entry owns it. Local marketplace behavior is unchanged.

## User Impact

Configured Codex plugin apps remain available on new OpenClaw threads when the local inventory snapshot has not been populated. Remote curated plugins can also be inspected and activated through the current Codex app-server wire contract, including when Codex returns a local curated marketplace before the remote catalog, while genuinely absent or inaccessible apps remain excluded fail-closed.

## Evidence

- Current head: `20ad6f60e2` on current `main`.
- Original focused Vitest run: 4 files passed, 53 tests passed.
- Review-fix focused Vitest run: 2 files passed, 13 tests passed.
- Focused `oxlint` passed for the 3 review-fix files.
- `oxfmt --check` passed for all 9 originally touched files; the 3 review-fix files were formatted with `oxfmt`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- The Codex app-server implementation was checked directly: `plugin/list` can return multiple local and remote marketplace entries, and `plugin/read` selects remote catalogs through `remoteMarketplaceName`.
- A local Slack/OpenClaw integration run on the pre-review implementation exercised the user-facing path: Calendar read and write/read-back passed; a Linear write produced an approval prompt and created exactly one issue after approval; an untouched Linear approval expired after 120 seconds and exact-title search returned zero issues.
- The final review adjustments added complete targeted pagination, repeated-cursor failure, remote install-ID coverage, and local-first/remote-plugin marketplace selection coverage. The live Slack flow was not rerun after these review fixes.
- AI-assisted change; I reviewed the implementation and understand the resulting app inventory and remote plugin activation behavior.
target.author: kevinslin
